title: Intelligence Efficiency, Ben Geist | Compile 26
summary: Ben Geist from Ramp argues that intelligence efficiency has diminishing returns with additional token spending. He proposes that better context rather than increased compute is the key to model efficiency, framing language models as entropy reduction machines where context quality matters more than scale.
writeup: Ben Geist's Compile 26 talk argues that intelligence efficiency comes from better context and entropy reduction, not from token expenditure alone. The central claim: smarter models do not automatically translate into smarter efficiency gains, and token-spend data from Ramp shows context optimization outperforming raw computational scaling. The takeaway reframes efficiency around what is fed into a model rather than how much compute is thrown at it.
